name: identical
description: Ellen Hopkins' verse novel about identical twin sisters Kaeleigh and Raeanne — a story of shared trauma, fractured identity, abuse, codependency, and the painful road to healing. Written in free verse from alternating perspectives.
useCase: Explore a powerful verse novel about twin identity, family trauma, and the long shadow of child abuse — told through the alternating voices of identical sisters.
knownRisks: The novel contains graphic depictions of child sexual abuse, self-harm, and substance abuse that may be triggering.
The verse format may not appeal to readers expecting traditional prose narrative.
Hopkins writes from the perspective of both abuser and abused, which some readers may find uncomfortable.
